behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with a 401 response; the body is not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at module handoff (HTTP handler + signature verifier + queue writer), not just hash math. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess required; in-process request + fake queue is enough. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because behavior text does not require real network/OS effects; Agentic/Workflow rejected because no AI step exists.
behaviors.test_description: Run handler with an in-process HTTP test request containing mismatched signature and assert 401 plus zero calls/writes to queue module.

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned to the sender.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: ordering bug appears across handler->queue boundary. REAL-INFRA: can be caught with local in-process persistence adapter and controlled timing; no real external network/subprocess required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because behavior text requires ordering, not real OS/network-specific behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real queue module backed by local test storage and instrument write completion; assert HTTP 200 is emitted only after write promise resolves/commit is visible.

behaviors.description: The handler returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing of the event to finish.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: asynchronous decoupling failure occurs between handler enqueue path and downstream worker module. REAL-INFRA: no need for real network/subprocess; slow downstream can be simulated in-process. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because behavior text does not require real OS/network artifacts, only non-blocking orchestration.
behaviors.test_description: Inject a downstream processor that blocks for a long time, send request through handler, assert response returns quickly while processor is still running.

behaviors.description: The response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing of earlier events is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: explicit wall-clock SLA under concurrency is sensitive to runtime scheduling, actual server behavior, and real HTTP timing; in-process fakes can hide latency contention. MOCK-HIDE: mocked timing often passes while real event-loop/resource contention fails. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: Integration rejected because behavior text explicitly requires wall-clock and concurrent slow earlier events, which are real runtime effects.
behaviors.test_description: Run the actual service process, generate concurrent real HTTP webhook traffic while earlier events trigger slow processing, and assert each new response time is <3s end-to-end.

behaviors.description: An event whose ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: dedupe correctness is at handler->idempotency store/queue interaction boundary. REAL-INFRA: can be validated with local wired modules and deterministic storage state; no real external OS/network needed. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because behavior text specifies idempotent write logic, not infrastructure-specific effects.
behaviors.test_description: Submit same event ID twice through handler against real local dedupe+queue modules; assert first write succeeds and second produces no additional queue record.

behaviors.description: When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: mapping queue-full condition to HTTP semantics crosses module boundary (queue -> handler response). REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess required; controlled queue-full signal in-process is sufficient. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because behavior text is about translation of an internal state to status/header, not real infrastructure timing.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler with queue module configured to return capacity-full error; assert response is 503 and includes valid Retry-After header.

behaviors.description: The persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit and rejects events that would exceed it.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-HIDE: a mocked queue would trivially pass; need the real queue implementation with its persistence layer. REAL-INFRA: this can be exercised with local DB/filesystem-backed queue in-process; no external API/subprocess required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because behavior text focuses on queue implementation correctness under configured limits, not OS/network/subprocess behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate real persistent queue backend with small capacity, enqueue until limit, then attempt one more enqueue and assert explicit rejection without exceeding stored count.
